rulings.Case Title: GLENNA WHINERY et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 26, 2008, line 1. DEFENDANT IMO INDUSTRIES, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant shifted the burden of proof based on factually devoid discovery. The objection to plaintiff's declaration is overruled. Plaintiff's evidence fails to create a triable issue of fact for there is no evidence that the products plaintiff describes contained asbestos. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: RUTH PARKER et al VS. KING SECURITY SERVICES INC ITS SUCCESSORS AND OR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joint Ntc Of Mtn And Joint Mtn For An Order(1) Preliminary Approving Class Action Settlement(2) Certifying The Class(3) Directing Mailing Of A Class Ntc Of Settlement (4) Scheduling A Final Approval Hearing (5) Appointing Representative Pltfs As Authorized Representatives Of The Class And Appointing Counsel Of Record As Class Counsel
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 26, 2008, line 2. DEFENDANT KING SECURITY SERVICES, INC'S Joint Motion For An Order (1) Preliminary Approving Class Action Settlement (2) Certifying The Class (3) Directing Mailing Of A Class Notice Of Settlement (4) Scheduling A Final Approval Hearing (5) Appointing Representative Plaintiffs as Authorized Representatives Of The Class And Appointing Counsel Of Record As Class Counsel is granted. Parties are to submit an order with a date for the final hearing and other relevant dates. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: PEGGY SNYDER VS. XTRONX, INC., A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 26, 2008, line 6. DEFENDANTS XTRONX, INC, XTRONX T2 LLC AND ERIC WONG'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The motion is granted striking subpart h of paragraph 37 as being inconsistent with the parties' agreement. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: STATE FARM GENERAL INSURANCAE COMPANY VS. FRANKE CONSUMER PRODUCTS, INC., et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 26, 2008, line 7. DEFENDANT FRANKE CONSUMER PRODUCTS, INC'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. The motion to strike is granted without leave to amend for plaintiff has not asserted a basis for an award of attorneys' fees. Doing so does not limit plaintiff's ability to recover fees based on discovery statutes. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: THERESA LYNN MURPHY VS. JAIYE ORISHAGBEMI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Vacate Default Judgment And Entry Of Default
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 26, 2008, line 14. DEFENDANT JAIYE ORISHAGBEMI'S Motion To Vacate Default Judgment And Entry Of Default is granted. Plaintiff had to obtain an order to post and serve the amended complaint; may serve an amended complaint by mail only after defendant appears. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: HOME LOAN EQUITY CORPORATION et al VS. LILLIE M HEREFORD et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 26, 2008, line 10. DEFENDANT LILLIE HEREFORD'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is denied. Plaintiff does not seek punitives or treble damages. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Jdugemnt
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 26, 2008, line 15. DEFENDANT MILLER VAUGHN'S Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment. A hearing required. It appears a complaint was not served after the 3 day notice. It is not clear what defendant's defense is to the non-payment of rent. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: EDWARD MURPHY VS. DENTA TADESSE et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 26, 2008, line 17. PLAINTIFF EDWARD MURPHY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The Court grants the request for a continuance. The matter is continued to September 2, 2008. Defendant to file opposition by August 29, 2008 by noon. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: GORDON BUTTERWORTH et al VS. LAURA CONTRERAS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 26, 2008, line 11. DEFENDANT LAURA CONTRERAS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled, 5 days to answer. The allegations of the complaint, if true, establish that the Rent Ordinance is not applicable. Only tenants are entitled to be advised of the Rent Ordinance. No ambiguity is created by reference to a terminated lease. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: GAETANI REALTY, INC. VS. ADAM NEWTON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 26, 2008, line 16. DEFENDANT CIRO MATARAZZO'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS is denied. The declaration of Golder provides facts that support effective substituted service of the complaint. =(302/PJM)
